In most cases, effect sizes were modestly attenuated when romance was controlled, and few remained statistically significant after FDR correction, consistent with the interpretation that the romantic relationship subsample is underpowered to reliably detect friendship effects of the magnitude observed in the full sample.

```{r model-8}
long_models_lag_mh_romsubset <- all_long %>%
  left_join(romance_cov, by = c("tagid", "day")) %>%
  filter(tagid %in% romantic_ids, !is.na(romance_daily)) %>%  # same subset as Model 6
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DSR_2") & !ends_with("_lag"),
    names_to = "friend_quality",
    values_to = "friend_score",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DSR_3"),
    names_to = "daily_mh_var",
    values_to = "daily_score",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dmh_lag_var = ifelse(str_detect(daily_mh_var, "lag"), "lag_score", "daily_score"),
    daily_mh_var = str_remove(daily_mh_var, "_lag")
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = dmh_lag_var, values_from = daily_score) %>%
  group_by(friend_quality, daily_mh_var) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(
      data,
      ~ lmer(
        daily_score ~ friend_score + lag_score + weekend + day +
          (1 + friend_score | tagid),
        data = .,
        REML = FALSE
      )
    ),
    summary = map(model, broom::tidy, conf.int = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-data, -model) %>%
  unnest(cols = summary) %>%
  filter(term == "friend_score") %>%
  mutate(
    p.adjusted = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr"),
    friend_quality = recode(friend_quality, !!!friend_quality_labels),
    daily_mh_var = recode(daily_mh_var, !!!mh_labels)
  ) %>%
  arrange(daily_mh_var, friend_quality)
```

## Model comparisons

### Comparing Model 6 and Model 7

```{r model-comparison}
model_comparison <- long_models_lag_mh_romsubset %>%
  select(friend_quality, daily_mh_var, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value, p.adjusted) %>%
  mutate(model = "Friendship Only") %>%
  bind_rows(
    long_models_lag_mh_romctrl %>%
      select(friend_quality, daily_mh_var, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value, p.adjusted) %>%
      mutate(model = "Friendship + Romance Controlled")
  )

model_comparison_display <- model_comparison %>%
  mutate(
    estimate = str_c(
      round(estimate, 2), " [", 
      round(conf.low, 2), ", ", 
      round(conf.high, 2), "]"
    ),
    p.value   = printp(p.value),
    p.adjusted = printp(p.adjusted)
  ) %>%
  select(friend_quality, daily_mh_var, model, estimate, p.value, p.adjusted) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(friend_quality, daily_mh_var),
    names_from = model,
    values_from = c(estimate, p.value, p.adjusted)
  ) %>%
  arrange(daily_mh_var, friend_quality)

names(model_comparison_display)

groups_comp <- model_comparison_display %>%
  mutate(row = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(daily_mh_var) %>%
  summarise(min = min(row), max = max(row), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(min)

model_comparison_display <- model_comparison_display %>%
  select(-daily_mh_var)

ncol(model_comparison_display)  
names(model_comparison_display)

model_comparison_display_final <- model_comparison_display %>%
  select(
    friend_quality,
    `estimate_Friendship Only`,
    `p.value_Friendship Only`, 
    `p.adjusted_Friendship Only`,
    `estimate_Friendship + Romance Controlled`,
    `p.value_Friendship + Romance Controlled`,
    `p.adjusted_Friendship + Romance Controlled`
  )

kable(
  model_comparison_display_final,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Comparison of friendship-only vs. romance-controlled models on the romantic relationship subset.",
  longtable = TRUE,
  col.names = c(
    "Friendship Quality",
    "b [95\\% CI]", "p", "p adj.",
    "b [95\\% CI]", "p", "p adj."
  ),
  align = "lcccccc",
  escape = FALSE
) %>%
  add_header_above(c(
    " " = 1,
    "Friendship Only" = 3,
    "Friendship + Romance Controlled" = 3
  )) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("scale_down", "repeat_header", "hold_position"),
    font_size = 9,
    full_width = FALSE
  ) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[1], groups_comp$min[1], groups_comp$max[1]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[2], groups_comp$min[2], groups_comp$max[2]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[3], groups_comp$min[3], groups_comp$max[3]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[4], groups_comp$min[4], groups_comp$max[4]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[5], groups_comp$min[5], groups_comp$max[5]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[6], groups_comp$min[6], groups_comp$max[6]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[7], groups_comp$min[7], groups_comp$max[7]) %>%
  group_rows(groups_comp$daily_mh_var[8], groups_comp$min[8], groups_comp$max[8]) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "2.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "2.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "1cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "1cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "2.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(6, width = "1cm") %>%
  column_spec(7, width = "1cm")
```

### Interaction

The interaction models tested whether the association between daily friendship experience and affect was moderated by concurrent daily romantic relationship experience — that is, whether the two relational contexts amplified or dampened one another's affective significance. A small number of interaction terms reached statistical significance (see Table 3 in the main manuscript), suggesting that for some outcomes, friendship and romantic experiences do not operate entirely independently. However, given the small subsample size and the number of models estimated, these interaction effects should be treated as exploratory and interpreted with caution pending replication in larger samples.

```{r model-romance-friendship}
# build romance composite separately - avoids cross-join
romance_composite <- all_long %>%
  select(tagid, day, starts_with("Q7_") & !ends_with("_lag")) %>%
  mutate(
    romance_score = rowMeans(across(starts_with("Q7_")), na.rm = TRUE),
    romance_score = ifelse(is.nan(romance_score), NA, romance_score)
  ) %>%
  select(tagid, day, romance_score)

test = all_long %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DSR_2") & !ends_with("_lag"),
    names_to = "friend_quality",
    values_to = "friend_score",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("DSR_3"),
    names_to = "daily_mh_var",
    values_to = "daily_score_raw",
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    dmh_lag_var  = ifelse(str_detect(daily_mh_var, "lag"), "lag_score", "daily_score"),
    daily_mh_var = str_remove(daily_mh_var, "_lag")
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = dmh_lag_var, values_from = daily_score_raw) %>%
  left_join(romance_composite, by = c("tagid", "day")) %>%
  mutate(person = "friend_score") %>%
  rename(score = friend_score) %>%
  group_by(friend_quality, daily_mh_var) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    model = map(
      data,
      ~ lmer(
        daily_score ~ score * romance_score +
          lag_score +
          weekend + day + 1 +
          (1 + score | tagid),
        data = .
      )
    ),
    summary = map(model, broom.mixed::tidy, conf.int = TRUE)
  )
```

```{r r-table-romance-friendship}
results_tbl <- test %>% 
  mutate(
    inter = map(model, ~ broom.mixed::tidy(.x, effects = "fixed") %>%
                  filter(term == "score:romance_score") %>%
                  transmute(
                    interaction_est = estimate,
                    interaction_se  = std.error,
                    interaction_df  = df,
                    interaction_p   = p.value
                  )
    ),
    slopes = map(model, ~ {
      friend_trend <- emtrends(.x, specs = ~ 1, var = "score") %>%
        summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
        as.data.frame() %>%
        transmute(
          friend_simple_slope_est = score.trend,
          friend_simple_slope_se  = SE,
          friend_simple_slope_df  = df,
          friend_simple_slope_p   = p.value
        )

      romance_trend <- emtrends(.x, specs = ~ 1, var = "romance_score") %>%
        summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
        as.data.frame() %>%
        transmute(
          romance_simple_slope_est = romance_score.trend,
          romance_simple_slope_se  = SE,
          romance_simple_slope_df  = df,
          romance_simple_slope_p   = p.value
        )

      bind_cols(friend_trend, romance_trend)
    })
  ) %>%
  select(friend_quality, daily_mh_var, inter, slopes) %>%
  unnest(c(inter, slopes)) %>%
  transmute(
    friend_quality,
    daily_mh_var,
    interaction_est,
    interaction_se,
    interaction_df,
    interaction_p,
    friend_simple_slope_est,
    friend_simple_slope_se,
    friend_simple_slope_df,
    friend_simple_slope_p,
    romance_simple_slope_est,
    romance_simple_slope_se,
    romance_simple_slope_df,
    romance_simple_slope_p
  )

results_tbl
```

```{r}
sig_star <- function(p){
  case_when(
    is.na(p)      ~ "",
    p <  .001     ~ "***",
    p <  .01      ~ "**",
    p <  .05      ~ "*",
    p <  .1       ~ ".",
    TRUE          ~ ""
  )
}

friend_map <- c(
  "DSR_2_1" = "supported",
  "DSR_2_2" = "rejected",
  "DSR_2_3" = "lonely",
  "DSR_2_4" = "secure",
  "DSR_2_5" = "jealous",
  "DSR_2_6" = "trusted",
  "DSR_2_7" = "valued",
  "DSR_2_8" = "stressed",
  "DSR_2_9" = "understood"
)

mh_map <- c(
  "DSR_3_1" = "depressed",
  "DSR_3_2" = "anxious",
  "DSR_3_3" = "sleepy",
  "DSR_3_4" = "self-confident",
  "DSR_3_5" = "stressed",
  "DSR_3_6" = "happy",
  "DSR_3_7" = "joyful",
  "DSR_3_8" = "excited"
)
```

```{r, results='asis'}

apa_results_tbl <- results_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    friend_quality = recode(friend_quality, !!!friend_map),
    daily_mh_var   = recode(daily_mh_var, !!!mh_map),
    across(ends_with("_est"), ~ round(.x, 3)),
    across(ends_with("_se"),  ~ round(.x, 3)),
    interaction_sig = sig_star(interaction_p),
    friend_sig      = sig_star(friend_simple_slope_p),
    romance_sig     = sig_star(romance_simple_slope_p)
  ) %>%
  select(
    friend_quality, daily_mh_var,
    interaction_est, interaction_se, interaction_sig,
    friend_simple_slope_est,  friend_simple_slope_se,  friend_sig,
    romance_simple_slope_est, romance_simple_slope_se, romance_sig
  ) %>%
  arrange(friend_quality, daily_mh_var) 

groups = apa_results_tbl %>% 
  mutate(row = row_number()) %>% 
  with_groups(friend_quality, summarise, min = min(row), max = max(row))

col_headers <- c(
  "Friendship Variable",
  "Daily Affect",
  "Interaction $b$", "SE", "",
  "Friendship $b$", "SE", "",
  "Romance $b$", "SE", ""
)

kable(
  apa_results_tbl,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  caption = "Interaction Effects and Simple Slopes Predicting Daily Affect",
  col.names = col_headers,
  align = "lccccccccc",
  escape = FALSE,
  longtable = TRUE   # keep this
) %>%
  add_header_above(c(
    " " = 2,
    "Interaction" = 3,
    "Friendship effect (at mean romance)" = 3,
    "Romance effect (at mean friendship)" = 3
  )) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("repeat_header", "hold_position"),  # no scale_down
    font_size = 7,
    full_width = TRUE
  ) %>%
  landscape() %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "1.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "1.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "0.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(5, width = "0.4cm") %>%
  column_spec(6, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(7, width = "0.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(8, width = "0.4cm") %>%
  column_spec(9, width = "1.2cm") %>%
  column_spec(10, width = "0.8cm") %>%
  column_spec(11, width = "0.4cm") %>%
  footnote(
    general = "Note. b = unstandardized estimate. “Friendship effect (at mean romance)” reflects the conditional effect of daily friendship experience on daily affect when daily romantic experience is held at its mean. “Romance effect (at mean friendship)” reflects the conditional effect of daily romantic experience when daily friendship experience is held at its mean. Significance codes: p <  .001~ ***, p <  .01 ~ **, p <  .05 ~ *",
general_title = "Note.",
    threeparttable = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE
  )
```

```{r}
cor.test(all_long$DSR_2_8, all_long$Q7_8)

ggplot(all_long, aes(x = DSR_2_8, y = Q7_8)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = .70) +
  labs(
    title = "Association Between Friendship Stress and Romantic Relationship Stress",
    x = "Friendship Stress",
    y = "Romantic Relationship Stress"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme_apa()
```

The scatter plots above illustrate the relationship between daily friendship stress and daily romantic relationship stress across participants. Although the two variables were positively correlated, the association was modest, indicating that these represent meaningfully distinct relational experiences rather than a single generalized stress response. This distinction supports the conceptual rationale for treating friendship and romantic relationship experiences as separate relational contexts in the present analyses.

```{r}
df3 <- all_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(DSR_2_8),
         !is.na(Q7_8),
         !is.na(DSR_3_5))

ggplot(df3, aes(x = DSR_2_8, y = Q7_8, color = DSR_3_5)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.75) +
  labs(
    title = "Friendship Stress, Romantic Stress, and Daily Affect Stress",
    x = "Friendship Stress",
    y = "Romantic Stress",
    color = "Daily Affect Stress"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme_apa()
```
